operating_state: Total operating revenues increased to $2.16 billion from $1.99 billion driven by property revenue growth and services expansion. U.S. & Canada segment delivered growth primarily from InSite acquisition contributions and T-Mobile master lease agreement impacts. Asia-Pacific revenue declined due to elevated India churn despite colocation growth, while Europe achieved strong growth from Orange acquisition progress. Operating income rose to $828.7 million from $736.8 million reflecting revenue growth partially offset by higher operating expenses.
cost_structure: Property and services costs of operations increased while depreciation, amortization and accretion expense rose driven by new site acquisitions and construction. Selling, general, administrative and development expense decreased primarily from reduced personnel costs, lower travel spending, and bad debt recovery in Asia-Pacific. Foreign currency denominated expenses comprised 49% of total operating expenses creating ongoing translation exposure.
material_events: Completed $1.4 billion registered offering of senior notes due 2026 and 2031 in March 2021 with proceeds used to repay existing revolving credit facility borrowings. InSite debt totaling $826.4 million was fully repaid in January 2021 with loss on retirement alongside prior term loan repayment. Signed definitive Telxius acquisition agreements for approximately 31,000 sites across six countries for $9.4 billion with European closings expected in Q2 2021. Appointed Sanjay Goel as Executive Vice President and President, Asia-Pacific.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in $323.7 million of other comprehensive loss with international operations representing 41% of revenues creating ongoing currency exposure. Indian Supreme Court AGR ruling created potential material financial impact on Indian tenants with significant tenant-related intangibles and tower assets representing exposure to tenant cash flow disruptions. COVID-19 pandemic continues creating operational uncertainties and potential demand impacts across global markets.
forward_outlook: Telxius acquisition expected to close in tranches through Q3 2021 subject to regulatory approvals with majority of European sites closing in Q2. Management projects 2021 capital expenditures including construction of communications sites globally. Plans to redeem remaining minority interests in ATC TIPL achieving full ownership subject to regulatory approval. Maintains $2.0 billion remaining stock repurchase authorization with pacing dependent on market conditions.
uncertainty_profile: Debt covenant compliance requires maintenance of total leverage ratios stepping up following Telxius acquisition completion. Established $7.3 billion financing capacity through delayed draw term loans and bridge commitment creating execution risk around closing conditions and timing across multiple jurisdictions. Securitization structures require minimum debt service coverage ratios with cash trap mechanisms if ratios fall below thresholds.
